prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model, assuming WMT's price follows a log-normal distribution. Key parameters used are an annualized drift (mean return) of 7% and an annualized volatility of 18%, which are typical for a stable, large-cap stock like Walmart over a short-to-medium term. The 20-day time horizon is translated to 20/252 years (assuming 252 trading days per year). The point estimate reflects the expected value given the drift, while the confidence intervals are calculated using standard deviations of the log-returns. This model assumes no unforeseen major company-specific news (e.g., earnings announcements, strategic shifts) or significant broad market events will drastically alter WMT's price trajectory within this period.
